sections.title: DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
sections.text: This Standard applies to the cobs, without the silk and anthers, of commercial varieties of baby corn (corn inflorescence) grown from Zea mays  L, of the Gramineae family, separated from  silk, husk and anthers, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Baby corn for industrial processing is excluded.
sections.retrieval_text:
DEFINITION OF PRODUCE
number=1
level=1
This Standard applies to the cobs, without the silk and anthers, of commercial varieties of baby corn (corn inflorescence) grown from Zea mays  L, of the Gramineae family, separated from  silk, husk and anthers, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Baby corn for industrial processing is excluded.

page_spans.text: This Standard applies to the cobs, without the silk and anthers, of commercial varieties of baby corn (corn inflorescence) grown from Zea mays  L, of the Gramineae family, separated from  silk, husk and anthers, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.  Baby corn for industrial processing is excluded.

sections.title: “Extra” Class
sections.text: The cobs of baby corn in this class must be well trimmed, free of husk, stalk and silk, intact and of superior quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commer cial type. They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text: The cobs of baby corn in this class must be well trimmed, free of husk and stalk and of good quality. They must be characteristic of the variety and/or commercial type. The following slight defects, however, may be allowed, provided these do not affect the general a ppearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package:

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING SIZING
sections.text:
Size is determined by the length of the cob of baby corn, in accordance with the following table:
Size Code Length (in centimetres)
A 5.0 - 7.0
B 7.0 - 9.0
C 9.0 - 12.0
For all sizes, the minimum width should not be less than 1.0 cm and the maximum width not more than 2.0 cm.

sections.title: PROVISIONS CONCERNING TOLERANCES
sections.text: Tolerances in respect of quality and size shall be allowed in each package for produce not satisfying the requirements of the class indicated.

sections.title: Class II
“Extra” Class
sections.text: Five percent by number or weight of cobs of baby corn not satisfying the requirements of the class, but meeting those of Class I or, exceptionally, coming within the tolerances of that class.

sections.title: Class I
sections.text:
Ten percent by number or weight of cobs of baby corn not satisfying the requirements of the class, but meeting those of Class II or, exceptionally, coming within the tolerances of that class.
In the case of cobs of baby corn with incompletely removed husk and stalk, only 5 % by number or weight of 0.5 cm long of the husk and stalk is allowed.

page_spans.text:

sections.title: Class II
sections.text:
Ten percent by number or weight of cobs of baby corn satisfyin g neither the requirements of the class nor the minimum requirements, with the exception of produce affected by rotting or any other deterioration rendering it unfit for consumption.
In the case of cobs of baby corn with incompletely removed husk and stal k, only 5% by number or weight of 0.5 cm long of the husk and stalk is allowed.

sections.title: UNIFORMITY
sections.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only cobs of baby corn of the same origin, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.
sections.retrieval_text:
5 PROVISIONS CONCERNING PRESENTATION > UNIFORMITY
number=5.1
level=2
The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only cobs of baby corn of the same origin, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

page_spans.text: The contents of each package must be uniform and contain only cobs of baby corn of the same origin, quality and size. The visible part of the contents of the package must be representative of the entire contents.

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text:
The cobs of baby corn must be packed in such a way as to protect the produce properly.  The materials used inside the package must be new 1, clean, and o f a quality such as to avoid causing any external or internal damage to the produce.  The use of materials, particularly of paper or stamps bearing trade specifications is allowed, provided the printing or labelling has been done with non-toxic ink or glue.
The cobs of baby corn shall be packed in each container in compliance with the Recommended International Code of Practice for Packaging and Transport of Fresh Fruits and Vegetables (CAC/RCP 44 -1995).

sections.title: CONSUMER PACKAGES
sections.text: In addition to the requirements of the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text: Each package must bear the following particulars, in letters grouped on the same side, legibly and indelibly marked, and visible from the outside, or in the documents accompanying the shipment. For produce transported in bulk, these particulars must appear on a document accompanying the goods.

sections.title: Description of Containers
sections.text: The containers shall meet the quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance characteristics to ensure suitable handling, shipping and preserving of the cobs o f baby corn. Packages must be free of all foreign matter and smell.

sentences.original: This Standard applies to the cobs, without the silk and anthers, of commercial varieties of baby corn (corn inflorescence) grown from Zea mays  L, of the Gramineae family, separated from  silk, husk and anthers, to be supplied fresh to the consumer, after preparation and packaging.

sentences.original: The cobs of baby corn in this class must be well trimmed, free of husk, stalk and silk, intact and of superior quality.

classification.sentence: They must be free of defects, with the exception of very slight superficial defects, provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package.
classification.pattern: [Main Obligation] + [Exception introduced by 'with the exception of'] + [Condition introduced by 'provided that']

components.obligation: They must be free of defects
components.exception: with the exception of very slight superficial defects
components.condition: provided these do not affect the general appearance of the produce, the quality, the keeping quality and presentation in the package

classification.justification: The sentence expresses a normative obligation to be defect-free but includes a specific exception and a condition qualifying that exception. This composite structure with both exception and conditional qualification does not strongly match any known templates, leading to a new pattern classification with high confidence in modality and function.
notes: The sentence combines an obligation with both an exception and a condition, which is a complex normative structure absent from the current template catalog.

sentences.original: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.

classification.sentence: If the produce is not visible from the outside, each package (or lot for produce presented in bulk) shall be labelled as to the name of the produce and may be labelled as to name of the variety.
classification.pattern: Condition clause + main clause combining 'shall' obligation and 'may' permission for labeling

pages.title: STANDARD FOR BABY CORN (CODEX STAN 188-1993)
pages.summary: A página contém a estrutura hierárquica principal do documento STANDARD FOR BABY CORN, identificando as seções 1 e 2 com seus respectivos subtítulos numerados até o nível 3. A seção 2 continua em páginas seguintes com subseções detalhadas. Os textos estão associados corretamente aos seus títulos, e a contagem total de nós reflete a complexidade hierárquica presente na página.

A página 2 contém a continuação da seção 2.2.3 Class II iniciada na página anterior, além das seções principais 3 e 4 com seus respectivos subtítulos numerados. O texto foi associado corretamente a cada nó estrutural, respeitando níveis hierárquicos e regras de continuidade. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado sem inclusão em texto das seções.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 188
pages.summary: A página 3 apresenta continuações e novos títulos das seções 4, 5 e 6. O cabeçalho 'CODEX STAN 188 Page 3 of 4' foi identificado. As seções 4.1.3 e 4.2 têm texto associado, enquanto as seções 5 e 6 funcionam como agrupadores estruturais com seus respectivos subtítulos numerados e textos associados preservados. Nenhuma seção continua de páginas anteriores ou segue adiante nesta página, garantindo uma estrutura clara para RAG.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 188
pages.summary: A página 4 contém conteúdo majoritariamente dos capítulos 6.2.1 a 8.2 do documento CODEX STAN 188, com identificação clara dos títulos e subtítulos. Observa-se continuidade da seção 6.2.1, indicando que já estava presente desde a página anterior. As seções 7 e 8 são claramente mapeadas com seus respectivos subtítulos, e a seção 8.2 indica continuidade para a próxima página. O cabeçalho e o título do documento foram identificados e excluídos do texto. A hierarquia reflete fielmente a estrutura numerada do documento para uso em RAG.
